title: DAVID — The Court Language Hidden in Plain Sight

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Archive-001: identical Archive interior — floor-to-ceiling manuscript shelves, long wooden worktable, open illuminated codex, cuneiform tablet, rolled papyrus, single brass desk lamp, same shelf blur depth, same eye-line to desk — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Documentary-Prestige-001: documentary prestige grade — rich midtones, restrained contrast, natural skin texture, subtle film grain, trustworthy broadcast gravitas, no beauty-filter smoothing, no Instagram saturation. Host seated at Archive worktable under neutral 5000K ambient key, speaks calmly to camera; motivated desk practical visible on codex surface only. Camera: slow gentle push-in medium. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Motivated desk practical in lower desk quadrant only — zero warm spill on host face, sweater, or shadows. Forbid yellow-green cast. Forbid blue-starved generation. Forbid magenta cast. Forbid global orange skin push. Motivated practical accent only — no unmotivated beauty dish, no fashion rim, lamp or window must be visible or implied. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "I am DAVID. Today, Classical Japanese — the court language of the Man'yōshū and The Tale of Genji, still hiding inside modern Japanese, unchanged on the surface but profoundly different underneath." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Opening shot, establish environment slowly.
shots.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, Classical Japanese — the court language of the Man'yōshū and The Tale of Genji, still hiding inside modern Japanese, unchanged on the surface but profoundly different underneath.

STYLE LOCK @Style-Documentary-Prestige-001: documentary prestige grade — rich midtones, restrained contrast, natural skin texture, subtle film grain, trustworthy broadcast gravitas, no beauty-filter smoothing, no Instagram saturation. Host leans slightly forward, traces finger across open document on desk, engaged with the evidence. Camera: medium — lean into evidence. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Motivated desk practical in lower desk quadrant only — zero warm spill on host face, sweater, or shadows. Forbid yellow-green cast. Forbid blue-starved generation. Forbid magenta cast. Forbid global orange skin push. Motivated practical accent only — no unmotivated beauty dish, no fashion rim, lamp or window must be visible or implied. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "In the Heian period — 794 to 1185 CE — the imperial court at Kyoto produced the world's first great prose novel. Murasaki Shikibu wrote Genji Monogatari in Classical Japanese around the year 1000. Sei Shōnagon wrote The Pillow Book at the same court. Both wrote in hiragana — the phonetic script then considered the register of women — while male courtiers wrote official documents in Chinese. The women's writing preserved the spoken vernacular." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.
shots.speech_text: In the Heian period — 794 to 1185 CE — the imperial court at Kyoto produced the world's first great prose novel. Murasaki Shikibu wrote Genji Monogatari in Classical Japanese around the year 1000. Sei Shōnagon wrote The Pillow Book at the same court. Both wrote in hiragana — the phonetic script then considered the register of women — while male courtiers wrote official documents in Chinese. The women's writing preserved the spoken vernacular.

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Archive-001: identical Archive interior — floor-to-ceiling manuscript shelves, long wooden worktable, open illuminated codex, cuneiform tablet, rolled papyrus, single brass desk lamp, same shelf blur depth, same eye-line to desk — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Documentary-Prestige-001: documentary prestige grade — rich midtones, restrained contrast, natural skin texture, subtle film grain, trustworthy broadcast gravitas, no beauty-filter smoothing, no Instagram saturation. Host settles back slightly, reflective scholarly mode — hands composed on desk, slight lean back. Camera: medium — slight pullback. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "Scholars have identified an eight-vowel system in the oldest Man'yōgana texts — where modern Japanese has five. The eighth-century scribes used distinct Chinese characters for sounds that later merged, leaving traces in the writing that only comparative analysis can now recover. Classical Japanese is not one stage but several, and the Heian literary tradition represents the language at its most fully documented peak." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.
shots.speech_text: Scholars have identified an eight-vowel system in the oldest Man'yōgana texts — where modern Japanese has five. The eighth-century scribes used distinct Chinese characters for sounds that later merged, leaving traces in the writing that only comparative analysis can now recover. Classical Japanese is not one stage but several, and the Heian literary tradition represents the language at its most fully documented peak.

provenance_card.title: DAVID · The Archive
